paper_title: The Last Temptation of William T. Tutte
summary: Conjecture 8 from arXiv:1912.07205 (Mohar and Singer, published in European Journal of Combinatorics 2021) asserts that every planar triangulation with a disconnected 4-coloring complex must contain at least one even-parity and one odd-parity component. The conjecture was posed as an open problem based on extensive computation, after the paper resolved Tutte's original question by constructing planar triangulations with arbitrarily many same-parity components. No follow-up work resolving this conjecture was found in the indexed literature through May 2026.
search_queries: planar triangulation 4-coloring complex disconnected parity components Mohar Singer arXiv 1912.07205
4-coloring complex planar triangulation even odd parity component graph theory 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
Kempe equivalence planar triangulation parity coloring complex cite "1912.07205" OR "Mohar Singer" 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The source paper was published as: European Journal of Combinatorics, vol. 91 (2021), article 103221, DOI:10.1016/j.ejc.2020.103221. A thematically adjacent paper arXiv:2101.04065 (Kempe equivalence of 4-critical planar graphs, showing every 4-critical planar graph has its 4-colorings forming a single Kempe class) was found but addresses a different structural question and does not directly resolve Conjecture 8. No follow-up found in indexed literature.
